title: Real A/B: same code-navigation task, with vs without Axil, at equal correctness
method: For each task, a grep/read-only agent and an Axil-CLI-only agent (both Opus 4.8, general-purpose) answer the same where/how question in byte-identical sandboxes; a judge scores each; only tasks BOTH answer correctly are counted. Tokens recomputed mechanically by re-executing consulted artifacts (scripts/context-ab-score.py). Steps = consulted tool roundtrips (~agent turns). Figures POOL two same-day runs per corpus: v2 (index at the old 100 KB default) and v3 (index at the fixed 512 KB default) - per-run splits included.
index: axil index structural proxies; v2 run @100KB default cap, v3 run @512KB (fixed default); no SCIP effect on these surfaces
sensitivity: fastapi pooled 93.9% includes one v3 task (fa10) where the unaided agent read fastapi/routing.py WHOLE: 63,311 tokens vs Axil 94 for the same verified answer. Excluding that single pair: 78.5%. Included because it is real behavior - whole-file reads of large files are exactly the discovery cost a code index removes.
notes: Pooled over 39 counted task-run pairs (flask 20, fastapi 19, django 20). Rate-limited/StructuredOutput-failed agent runs excluded by the both-correct rule (infra failures, not task failures). Discovery costs are heavy-tailed; per-run reductions ranged flask 41.8-68.5%, fastapi 75.4-98.2%, django 71.9-77.7%.
